blurb: Promotes fair and equitable access to the civil and criminal justice system -- administering courts, prosecuting criminal offences, resolving family disputes, enforcing support orders, overseeing legal aid, protecting property rights, registering land titles, and investigating sudden or unexplained deaths.

goals.title: OUTCOME 1
goals.short_name: Albertans' rights and interests are protected
goals.description: The ministry protects Albertans' rights by challenging unconstitutional federal legislation, advocating for responsible firearms policy, safeguarding property rights through land and personal property registries, and administering fair administrative penalty processes.

metrics.name: Possession and Acquisition Licences processed within target timeline

main_services.name: Get a firearms licence or authorization

supporting.name: Apply for a Possession and Acquisition Licence (PAL)
supporting.desc: Apply for a new PAL or Restricted PAL to legally possess and acquire firearms. Requires completing the Canadian Firearms Safety Course. Applications reviewed by the Alberta Chief Firearms Office before federal processing.

legislation.full_name: Alberta Firearms Act  -  Provincial Administration
Firearms Act (federal)  -  s.5-12: Eligibility and Licensing

mandate_tags.tip: Defend Albertans' right to lawful and safe possession of firearms

supporting.name: Renew a firearms licence
supporting.desc: Renew your PAL or RPAL before it expires (every 5 years).

legislation.full_name: Firearms Act (federal)  -  s.5-12: Eligibility and Licensing

mandate_tags.tip: Defend Albertans' right to lawful and safe possession of firearms

supporting.name: Take a firearms safety course
supporting.desc: Complete the Canadian Firearms Safety Course (CFSC) or Canadian Restricted Firearms Safety Course (CRFSC), required before applying for a licence.

supporting.name: Register or transfer a firearm
supporting.desc: Register restricted or prohibited firearms, or complete the legal process to transfer ownership of a firearm.
supporting.users: Firearms owners buying, selling, or registering

legislation.full_name: Firearms Act (federal)  -  Registration and Transfer

supporting.name: Get approved to operate a shooting club or range
supporting.desc: Apply for government approval to operate a shooting club or firing range in Alberta.

supporting.name: Register a land title document or plan
supporting.desc: Register documents that create or terminate legal rights in property through the Torrens System land registry.

channels.label: Mail or courier to Land Titles Office

legislation.full_name: Land Titles Act  -  Document Registration

supporting.name: Search land title records online
supporting.desc: Look up land title certificates, property ownership, registered interests, and plans.

legislation.full_name: Land Titles Act  -  Public Record Access

relationships.service: Register a land title document or plan

supporting.name: Register, search, or change a personal property lien
supporting.desc: Register a lien on personal property as loan security, search the registry before buying items, and change or discharge existing registrations.

legislation.full_name: Personal Property Security Act  -  Lien Registration

relationships.service: Search or register a personal property lien

supporting.name: Pay or dispute a traffic ticket
supporting.desc: Pay traffic fines, enter a plea, request a prosecutor review, or request a trial date. Available 24/7 online.
supporting.users: Drivers who received a traffic ticket

legislation.full_name: Provincial Offences Procedure Act  -  Traffic Ticket Processing
Traffic Safety Act  -  Part 5: General Operation of Vehicles

mandate_tags.tip: Strengthen justice system and expand virtual and online accessibility

supporting.name: Pay or dispute an impaired driving penalty through SafeRoads Alberta
supporting.desc: Pay administrative penalties, request more time to pay, dispute Notices of Administrative Penalty, or apply for vehicle seizure reviews related to impaired driving.
supporting.users: Drivers who received Immediate Roadside Sanctions

legislation.full_name: Provincial Administrative Penalties Act  -  Impaired Driving Sanctions

supporting.name: Enroll in an ignition interlock program
supporting.desc: Participate in a mandatory ignition interlock program after an impaired driving suspension or Criminal Code conviction.
supporting.users: Drivers with impaired driving suspensions or convictions

legislation.full_name: Provincial Administrative Penalties Act  -  Ignition Interlock Programs

supporting.name: File a claim for injuries from an uninsured or hit-and-run driver
supporting.desc: Get compensation for injuries caused by an uninsured or unknown driver. Maximum combined payout is $200,000 per accident. Must notify within 90 days for hit-and-run.
supporting.users: Victims of motor vehicle accidents involving uninsured or unknown drivers

legislation.full_name: Motor Vehicle Accident Claims Act  -  Claims Process

supporting.name: Resolve an unpaid fine under enforcement
supporting.desc: Resolve fines that have been referred to the Fines Enforcement Program, including options for payment plans or community work.

main_services.name: Get a professional licence or designation

supporting.name: Apply for a security service worker licence
supporting.desc: Get licensed to work as a security guard, alarm responder, patrol dog handler, loss prevention worker, or bodyguard.

legislation.full_name: Security Services and Investigators Act  -  Licensing

supporting.name: Apply for a private investigator licence
supporting.desc: Get licensed to work as a private or in-house investigator in Alberta.

legislation.full_name: Security Services and Investigators Act  -  Licensing

supporting.name: Apply for a locksmith licence
supporting.desc: Get licensed to work as a locksmith in Alberta. Includes locksmith equipment sales and automotive lock bypass licences.

legislation.full_name: Security Services and Investigators Act  -  Licensing

supporting.name: Apply for a security, investigation, or locksmith business licence
supporting.desc: Get a business licence to operate a security, investigation, or locksmith company.
supporting.users: Business owners in security, investigation, or locksmith industries

legislation.full_name: Security Services and Investigators Act  -  Business Licensing

relationships.service: File a complaint about a security, locksmith, or investigation service

supporting.name: Apply for a body armour permit
supporting.desc: Get a permit to buy, transport, or wear body armour.

legislation.full_name: Security Services and Investigators Act  -  Body Armour Permits

supporting.name: File a complaint about a security, locksmith, or investigation service
supporting.desc: Report concerns about a licensed security, locksmith, or investigative firm or worker.

legislation.full_name: Security Services and Investigators Act  -  Complaints

relationships.service: Apply for a security, investigation, or locksmith business licence

supporting.name: Apply to become a commissioner for oaths
supporting.desc: Apply for new or renewal appointment as Commissioner for Oaths.

legislation.full_name: Notaries and Commissioners Act  -  Commissioners for Oaths

supporting.name: Apply for appointment as a notary public
supporting.desc: Apply to be appointed as a notary public to administer oaths, certify documents, and witness document execution.

legislation.full_name: Notaries and Commissioners Act  -  Appointment and Duties

supporting.name: Authenticate a document for use in another country
supporting.desc: Get an apostille or authentication certificate for documents that need to be recognized in another jurisdiction. $25 per document.
supporting.users: Individuals and businesses needing documents recognized abroad

foundational_services.name: Challenge unconstitutional federal legislation
foundational_services.desc: Legal challenges to federal laws that may infringe on Alberta's constitutional jurisdiction, including under the Alberta Sovereignty within a United Canada Act.

mandate_tags.tip: Use every legal means to overturn harmful federal legislation

foundational_services.users: Alberta residents (represented by the Minister of Justice)

foundational_services.name: Administer the Canadian Firearms Program for Alberta
foundational_services.desc: The Alberta Chief Firearms Office reviews PAL applications, conducts investigations, and makes regulatory decisions. Investment of $8.6M in 2025-26.

legislation.full_name: Alberta Firearms Act

mandate_tags.tip: Defend Albertans' right to lawful and safe possession of firearms

foundational_services.users: Firearms owners, applicants, and dealers in Alberta

goals.title: OUTCOME 2
goals.short_name: Alberta's justice system is fair and accessible
goals.description: Albertans deserve a justice system that is fair and accessible. The ministry is increasing online services, implementing pre-charge assessment, streamlining court processes, and expanding virtual hearings.

metrics.name: Court of Justice lead time to trial for serious and violent matters
Investment in online services, digital platforms and video conferencing
SafeRoads program for alcohol/drug-related driving matters

main_services.name: Resolve a civil dispute through the courts

supporting.name: File a civil claim or application in court
supporting.desc: Start a civil action by filing documents with the Court of Justice or Court of King's Bench.
supporting.users: Individuals, businesses, organizations involved in civil disagreements

legislation.full_name: Court of King's Bench Act  -  Civil Filing and Procedure

mandate_tags.tip: Strengthen justice system and expand virtual and online accessibility

supporting.name: Search court records online
supporting.desc: Search for civil, family, divorce, bankruptcy, appeals, surrogate, and criminal matters by party name or file number. $25 per search.

channels.label: eServices: Court of Justice civil search

legislation.full_name: Court of King's Bench Act  -  Records and Information

mandate_tags.tip: Strengthen justice system and expand virtual and online accessibility

relationships.service: File a civil claim or application in court

supporting.name: Order a courtroom transcript
supporting.desc: Order a written transcript of court proceedings for legal, insurance, or personal purposes. Requires a Justice Digital account.

mandate_tags.tip: Strengthen justice system and expand virtual and online accessibility

supporting.name: Appeal a court decision
supporting.desc: File an appeal to the Court of King's Bench or the Court of Appeal. Court of Appeal civil appeal filing fee: $600.

channels.label: eServices: Court of Appeal filing fees

legislation.full_name: Court of King's Bench Act  -  Appeals Jurisdiction

supporting.name: Request civil claims mediation
supporting.desc: Use a mediator to negotiate a resolution to a civil claim in the Court of Justice instead of going to trial.

supporting.name: Apply to administer a deceased person's estate
supporting.desc: Apply to the Court of King's Bench for a Grant of Probate or Grant of Administration to manage a deceased person's estate.
supporting.users: Executors, family members of deceased, lawyers

legislation.full_name: Estate Administration Act  -  Grants of Probate and Administration

supporting.name: Get help preparing court forms
supporting.desc: Access assistance with court form preparation through court forms information coordinators at courthouses.

supporting.name: Apply to waive a court filing fee
supporting.desc: Request a fee waiver if you cannot afford court filing fees based on household income.

supporting.name: Request a review of a lawyer's fees
supporting.desc: Apply to the court for a review if you believe you were overcharged by a lawyer.
supporting.users: Clients who believe they were overcharged

supporting.name: Enforce a civil judgment or debt
supporting.desc: Engage a civil enforcement agency for property seizure, garnishment, or eviction to enforce a court judgment.

legislation.full_name: Civil Enforcement Act  -  Seizure and Enforcement

supporting.name: Respond to a jury summons
supporting.desc: Complete your juror questionnaire online or by mail. Required by law. Jury pay is $50/day.
supporting.users: Alberta residents summoned for jury duty

legislation.full_name: Jury Act  -  Juror Selection and Service

supporting.name: Claim jury duty expenses
supporting.desc: Submit expense claims for meals, mileage, parking, transit, and accommodations incurred during jury service.
supporting.users: Jurors who served on a trial

legislation.full_name: Jury Act  -  Juror Compensation

supporting.name: Apply for Legal Aid
supporting.desc: Apply for legal representation at reduced or no cost for criminal, family, and civil matters. Means test required.
supporting.users: Low-income Albertans facing criminal charges or family law matters

supporting.name: Get emergency legal advice while in custody
supporting.desc: Access immediate legal advice through the Brydges Program if you are in custody or detained, regardless of income.

supporting.name: Get free family law information at a Family Law Information Centre
supporting.desc: Get free information about child/spousal support, parenting, guardianship, custody, property division, protection orders, and common-law rights at FLIC locations. Cannot give legal advice.

channels.label: FLIC locations (Calgary, Edmonton, Grande Prairie, Lethbridge, Medicine Hat, Red Deer)

supporting.name: Get support navigating court as an Indigenous person
supporting.desc: Access culturally relevant court support through the Indigenous Court Work Program in criminal, youth, or family court. Includes Edmonton Indigenous Court, Calgary Indigenous Court, and St. Paul Healing to Wellness Court.
supporting.users: Indigenous people involved in the court system

supporting.name: Access family law form kits for self-representation
supporting.desc: Download court forms and information packages for family law matters including divorce, custody, and support.
supporting.users: Self-represented litigants in family law matters

main_services.name: File a complaint about policing or the justice process

supporting.name: File a policing complaint or compliment
supporting.desc: Complain about police conduct or commend an officer. Complaints go to the police service's professional standards unit.
supporting.users: Members of the public who interacted with police

supporting.name: Appeal a police complaint decision
supporting.desc: Appeal a complaint decision to the Law Enforcement Review Board (LERB), an independent civilian oversight body. Must appeal within 30 days.
supporting.users: Complainants dissatisfied with a police complaint outcome

supporting.name: Complain about treatment during the criminal justice process
supporting.desc: File complaints about how you were treated by organizations involved in the criminal justice process (police, RCMP, court services, Crown prosecution).

foundational_services.name: Prosecute criminal offences
foundational_services.desc: Alberta Crown Prosecution Service conducts criminal prosecutions across 13 offices. Handles Criminal Code, Youth Criminal Justice Act, and provincial statute matters.

mandate_tags.tip: Advocate for federal bail reform and Criminal Code changes

foundational_services.name: Administer court scheduling and operations
foundational_services.desc: Court and Justice Services manages daily court operations including scheduling, docket management, courtroom proceedings, and adjournment processing across all Alberta courts.

legislation.full_name: Court of Justice Act  -  Court Administration
Court of King's Bench Act  -  Court Administration

mandate_tags.tip: Strengthen justice system and expand virtual and online accessibility

foundational_services.name: Process pre-charge assessments
foundational_services.desc: Prosecutors review charges before they are laid by police, ensuring only viable matters enter the court system.

mandate_tags.tip: Strengthen justice system and expand virtual and online accessibility

foundational_services.name: Operate drug treatment courts
foundational_services.desc: Judicially supervised treatment and rehabilitation for non-violent offenders with addiction-motivated crime. Investment of up to $5M annually.
foundational_services.users: Non-violent offenders with substance use disorders (by court referral)

foundational_services.name: Investigate sudden, unexpected, or unexplained deaths
foundational_services.desc: The Office of the Chief Medical Examiner conducts forensic death investigations, autopsies, and toxicology analysis. Approximately 20,000 deaths investigated annually.

legislation.full_name: Fatality Inquiries Act  -  Death Investigation Authority

goals.title: OUTCOME 3
goals.short_name: Albertans are supported in their interactions with the justice system
goals.description: The ministry supports individuals and families through family dispute resolution, victim services, maintenance enforcement, therapeutic courts, restorative justice, guardianship and trusteeship services, and modernized death investigations.

metrics.name: Family matters resolved by Alberta Justice dispute resolution programs

main_services.name: Resolve a family dispute without going to court

supporting.name: Assess your family law options online
supporting.desc: Use the Family Resolution Hub, a free online tool, to understand your family law options and get a personalized summary of next steps. No login or personal information required.
supporting.users: Parents, guardians dealing with family law matters

legislation.full_name: Family Law Act  -  Family Dispute Resolution

supporting.name: Complete the Parenting After Separation course
supporting.desc: Take the required 6-hour PAS course before filing divorce or Family Law Act applications. Mandatory in Edmonton, Calgary, Red Deer. High-conflict version (PASHC) also available (3 hours).
supporting.users: Parents filing for divorce or family law orders

legislation.full_name: Family Law Act  -  Pre-Court Requirements

supporting.name: Access free family mediation
supporting.desc: Free mediation for parents, grandparents, guardians to resolve parenting plans and child support. One parent must have gross income of $60,000 or less.
supporting.users: Parents and guardians in parenting disputes

legislation.full_name: Family Law Act  -  Dispute Resolution

supporting.name: Resolve a child support dispute
supporting.desc: Meet with a family law lawyer to help reach a child support agreement. Free program available in 9 locations.
supporting.users: Parents needing to establish or adjust child support

legislation.full_name: Family Law Act  -  Child Support

supporting.name: Register for automatic child support recalculation
supporting.desc: Have child support recalculated annually based on current income tax information without returning to court. Either parent can register.

legislation.full_name: Family Law Act  -  Child Support Recalculation

supporting.name: Get family court counsellor assistance
supporting.desc: Meet with a family court counsellor for help with parenting disputes and court preparation. Mandatory before filing family law actions in Edmonton, Calgary, Red Deer (unless represented by a lawyer).

legislation.full_name: Family Law Act  -  Court Assistance

supporting.name: Mediate a Children's Services dispute
supporting.desc: Get mediation help when your family is involved with child intervention services.

supporting.name: Meet with a clinician for a high-conflict parenting dispute
supporting.desc: Access the Brief Conflict Intervention program for a meeting with a clinician to help manage interactions and reduce conflict.

supporting.name: Apply for a parenting or custody order
supporting.desc: Apply to the court to establish guardianship decisions and parenting time arrangements for your child.

legislation.full_name: Family Law Act  -  Part 2: Guardianship and Parenting

supporting.name: Apply for child or spousal support
supporting.desc: Apply for a court order for child support or spousal/partner support based on parental income and child needs.

legislation.full_name: Family Law Act  -  Part 4: Child Support and Part 5: Spousal/Partner Support

supporting.name: Get a divorce
supporting.desc: Start the divorce process, including filing for divorce, dividing property, and resolving custody and support.

legislation.full_name: Divorce Act (federal)  -  Divorce and Corollary Relief
Family Law Act  -  Non-Divorce Matters

supporting.name: Apply for child guardianship
supporting.desc: Apply for legal guardianship of a child you've cared for at least 6 months, if neither a parent nor existing guardian.

legislation.full_name: Family Law Act  -  Part 2: Guardianship

supporting.name: Change a court order for child support
supporting.desc: Apply to vary an existing child support order when circumstances have changed.

legislation.full_name: Family Law Act  -  Child Support Variation

main_services.name: Get help collecting or paying court-ordered support

supporting.name: Set up enforcement for a support order
supporting.desc: Register a court-ordered child, spousal, or partner support order for collection and enforcement. Either payor or recipient can register. Enforcement powers include collection from employers, banks, and federal government, and restricting driver's licences and passports.

channels.label: Mail to 7th Floor, 10365 97 St NW, Edmonton

legislation.full_name: Maintenance Enforcement Act  -  Registration and Enforcement

supporting.name: Track and manage your support payments online
supporting.desc: View account status, track payment history, update your file, and contact your compliance officer.

legislation.full_name: Maintenance Enforcement Act  -  Account Management

supporting.name: Enforce support payments from outside Alberta
supporting.desc: Manage enforcement when the payor or recipient lives outside Alberta through reciprocal arrangements with other provinces and countries.
supporting.users: Support payors or recipients in different jurisdictions

legislation.full_name: Maintenance Enforcement Act  -  Reciprocal Enforcement

supporting.name: Get an emergency protection order
supporting.desc: Immediate protection in urgent family violence situations. Police can apply on your behalf. Can be same-day. Reviewed by judge within 9 working days. No filing fees.
supporting.users: Victims of family violence in urgent situations

channels.label: Any Court of Justice during business hours

legislation.full_name: Protection Against Family Violence Act  -  Emergency Protection Orders

supporting.name: Apply for a restraining order or other protection order
supporting.desc: Multiple types of protective orders for different situations: family violence restraining orders (no filing fee), King's Bench protection orders, peace bonds (non-criminal, up to one year), and exclusive home possession orders.
supporting.users: Victims of violence, people feeling threatened

legislation.full_name: Family Law Act  -  Part 7: Protection Orders

supporting.name: Request information about a partner's violent history (Clare's Law)
supporting.desc: People at risk of domestic violence can request information about a partner's violent past. Two paths: 'Right to Ask' (citizen initiates) and 'Right to Know' (police initiate). Disclosure takes approximately 4 weeks.
supporting.users: People in intimate partner relationships who feel at risk

legislation.full_name: Disclosure to Protect Against Domestic Violence (Clare's Law) Act

supporting.name: Call the Family Violence Info Line
supporting.desc: 24/7 support line for anyone experiencing or concerned about family violence. Available in 170 languages.
supporting.users: Anyone experiencing or concerned about family violence

supporting.name: Apply for the Escaping Abuse Benefit
supporting.desc: Get emergency financial help to establish a household after leaving an abusive situation.

supporting.name: Get a Safer Spaces Certificate to break a lease
supporting.desc: Break a residential lease without penalty when fleeing domestic violence.

supporting.name: Apply for a human trafficking protection order
supporting.desc: Get a 3-year court order to protect trafficking survivors from their traffickers.

main_services.name: Get help as a victim of crime

supporting.name: Apply for financial assistance as a crime victim
supporting.desc: Apply for emergency assistance, residential security, counselling, medical treatment, and other supports. Crime must be reported to police. Apply within 2 years.
supporting.users: Victims of serious Criminal Code offences

legislation.full_name: Victims of Crime and Public Safety Act  -  Victim Assistance

supporting.name: Submit a victim impact statement
supporting.desc: Prepare and submit a statement describing how a crime affected you, for the judge to consider before sentencing.
supporting.users: Victims of crime before offender sentencing

channels.label: Courthouse clerk, victim services, or police

legislation.full_name: Victims of Crime and Public Safety Act  -  Victim Rights

supporting.name: Submit a community impact statement
supporting.desc: Describe the impact of a crime on your community for the judge to consider before sentencing.

supporting.name: Request victim restitution from an offender
supporting.desc: Request court-ordered compensation from an offender for crime-related expenses. Must submit before sentencing.
supporting.users: Victims of crime where the offender has been convicted

channels.label: Crown Prosecution, victim services, or police

legislation.full_name: Victims of Crime and Public Safety Act  -  Restitution

supporting.name: Find a victim services organization near you
supporting.desc: Locate a victim serving organization in your area. Four regional societies align with RCMP districts.

legislation.full_name: Victims of Crime and Public Safety Act  -  Victim Services

supporting.name: Get reimbursed for attending court as a victim
supporting.desc: Get reimbursed for travel expenses to attend court proceedings as a crime victim or family of a homicide victim.
supporting.users: Crime victims, families of homicide victims

supporting.name: File a complaint about victim services
supporting.desc: File complaints about victim serving organizations or the Victims of Crime Assistance Program.

supporting.name: Get support as a family of a missing or murdered Indigenous person
supporting.desc: Access dedicated support and information through the Family Information Liaison Unit.
supporting.users: Families of missing or murdered Indigenous people

main_services.name: Protect your rights and plan for the future

supporting.name: Apply for guardianship or trusteeship of an adult
supporting.desc: When an adult cannot make personal or financial decisions, family can apply to court for guardianship (personal decisions) or trusteeship (financial decisions). The Office of the Public Guardian and Trustee assists and acts as guardian/trustee of last resort.
supporting.users: Family members of adults who lack capacity

legislation.full_name: Adult Guardianship and Trusteeship Act  -  Guardianship and Trusteeship Orders

supporting.name: Get a capacity assessment done
supporting.desc: Have a qualified professional assess whether an adult can make decisions independently.
supporting.users: Families or legal representatives of adults with suspected decision-making challenges

legislation.full_name: Adult Guardianship and Trusteeship Act  -  Capacity Assessment

supporting.name: Explore decision-making alternatives to guardianship
supporting.desc: Consider co-decision-making, supported decision-making, and specific decision-making as less restrictive alternatives to full guardianship or trusteeship.
supporting.users: Families of adults who need decision-making support

legislation.full_name: Adult Guardianship and Trusteeship Act  -  Decision-Making Alternatives

supporting.name: Make a personal directive or enduring power of attorney
supporting.desc: Create a personal directive (names an agent for personal decisions if you become incapacitated) and/or enduring power of attorney (gives authority over financial decisions).

legislation.full_name: Personal Directives Act  -  Personal Decision-Making
Powers of Attorney Act  -  Financial Decision-Making

supporting.name: File a complaint about a guardian, trustee, or agent
supporting.desc: Report concerns about someone acting as guardian, trustee, or agent for a vulnerable adult.
supporting.users: Concerned family members, community members, professionals

legislation.full_name: Adult Guardianship and Trusteeship Act  -  Complaints

main_services.name: Resolve a dispute outside of court

supporting.name: Resolve a residential tenancy dispute
supporting.desc: Use the Residential Tenancy Dispute Resolution Service as a faster alternative to court for landlord-tenant disputes. Claims under $100,000 within 2 years.

legislation.full_name: Residential Tenancies Act  -  Dispute Resolution

supporting.name: Resolve a condominium dispute
supporting.desc: Use the Condominium Dispute Resolution Tribunal for guided negotiation, mediation, or adjudication of condominium-related disputes.
supporting.users: Condo owners, condo boards, property managers

supporting.name: Participate in a restorative justice program
supporting.desc: Voluntary, confidential process where victims, offenders, family members, and community meet to address harm and promote meaningful resolution.

supporting.name: Report an unexpected or unexplained death
supporting.desc: Notify the Office of the Chief Medical Examiner of a sudden, unexpected, or unexplained death. Medical Investigator Unit available 24/7.
supporting.users: Public, physicians, healthcare professionals, law enforcement

legislation.full_name: Fatality Inquiries Act  -  Death Reporting

supporting.name: Request death investigation documents
supporting.desc: Obtain documents related to a completed death investigation by the Office of the Chief Medical Examiner.
supporting.users: Families of deceased, lawyers, insurance companies

legislation.full_name: Fatality Inquiries Act  -  Death Investigation and Documents

supporting.name: Request a public fatality inquiry
supporting.desc: Request through the Fatality Review Board that a public inquiry be held into a death investigated by the Chief Medical Examiner.
supporting.users: Family members of deceased, interested parties

legislation.full_name: Fatality Inquiries Act  -  Public Fatality Inquiries

cross_cutting: Court and Justice Services contact centre (1-855-738-4747)

mandate_legend.text: Use every legal means to overturn harmful federal legislation (C-69, emissions cap, clean electricity regs, C-48, carbon tax, plastics, C-59, fertilizer regs, EV mandate)
Introduce MAID legislation with safeguards; prohibit MAID based solely on mental illness
mandate_legend.mapping: -> No direct citizen service  |  Legislative initiative

mandate_legend.text: Strengthen justice system: increase applications judges, support staff, innovative approaches, expand virtual and online accessibility
mandate_legend.mapping: -> File court documents; Handle a traffic ticket online; Order a courtroom transcript; Foundational: court operations

mandate_legend.text: Defend Albertans' right to lawful and safe possession of firearms and affirm right of self-defence
mandate_legend.mapping: -> Get a firearms licence or authorization; Foundational: administer firearms program

mandate_legend.text: Introduce free speech legislation for all provincially regulated professionals
mandate_legend.mapping: -> No direct citizen service  |  Legislative initiative

mandate_legend.text: Advocate for federal bail reform and Criminal Code changes for public safety
mandate_legend.mapping: -> Foundational: prosecute criminal offences  |  Cross-ministry: Public Safety

mandate_legend.text: Remove National Capital Region residency requirements for federal court judges
mandate_legend.mapping: -> No direct citizen service  |  Federal advocacy

mandate_legend.text: Review and reform DEI hiring in Alberta public institutions to ensure merit-based practices
mandate_legend.mapping: -> No direct citizen service  |  Cross-ministry initiative

mandate_legend.text: Work with Infrastructure on courthouse assessment, renewal, and construction (supporting)
Defend Alberta's energy and natural resource interests against federal overreach (supporting)
mandate_legend.mapping: -> Foundational: constitutional challenges  |  Cross-ministry: Energy, Environment

mandate_legend.text: Create a policing priority framework with Public Safety (supporting)
mandate_legend.mapping: -> No direct citizen service  |  Cross-ministry: Public Safety

mandate_legend.text: Assess need for legislation protecting academic freedom and freedom of expression at post-secondary institutions (supporting, with Advanced Education)
mandate_legend.mapping: -> No direct citizen service  |  Cross-ministry: Advanced Education

mandate_legend.text: Support implementation of Canadian citizenship identifier on driver's licences and provincial ID cards to bolster election security (supporting, with Service Alberta)
mandate_legend.mapping: -> No direct citizen service  |  Cross-ministry: Service Alberta
